description: Lock corrected Energy Systems chronology for geothermal, pumped hydro, and modern wind-power nodes.

checks.reason: Broad geothermal power is anchored to early dry-steam electricity generation at Larderello.
Early dry-steam geothermal power did not require modern deep-drilling technology.
Geothermal power should remain upstream of the broader renewable-energy systems node.
The pumped-hydro node follows DOE's global first-known PSH use cases in Italy and Switzerland in the 1890s, not only the later U.S. Rocky River milestone.
Modern large-scale wind power is anchored to the Smith-Putnam grid-connected turbine.
Wind farms are anchored to the Crotched Mountain multi-turbine installation.
Renewable microgrids are scoped to the modern CERTS-style microgrid concept rather than early centralized grids.
